@@ -197,6 +197,48 @@ final class OnboardingFlowTests: XCTestCase {
         controller.handleSetupDisappear()
     }
 
+    func testEnableScreenRecordingShowsRelaunchSpawnFailure() {
+        let screenRecordingPermission = OnboardingScreenRecordingPermission(initialState: .denied)
+        let settingsOpener = OnboardingSystemSettingsOpener()
+        let relaunchScheduler = OnboardingRelaunchScheduler()
+        relaunchScheduler.error = OnboardingRelaunchTestError(description: "launchd rejected request")
+        let controller = makeController(
+            screenRecordingPermission: screenRecordingPermission,
+            settingsOpener: settingsOpener,
+            relaunchScheduler: relaunchScheduler
+        )
+
+        controller.advanceFromWelcome()
+        controller.handleSetupAppear()
+        controller.enableScreenRecording()
+
+        XCTAssertEqual(
+            controller.setupErrorMessage,
+            "Failed to relaunch Ghost Pepper: launchd rejected request"
+        )
+        XCTAssertEqual(screenRecordingPermission.requestAccessCallCount, 0)
+        XCTAssertEqual(settingsOpener.openedPanes, [])
+
+        controller.handleSetupDisappear()
+    }
+
+    func testShellOnboardingRelaunchSchedulerUsesOpenNAfterDelay() throws {
+        let launcher = OnboardingRelaunchLauncher()
+        let scheduler = ShellOnboardingRelaunchScheduler(
+            isRunningTests: false,
+            bundleURLProvider: { URL(fileURLWithPath: "/Applications/Ghost Pepper.app") },
+            launcher: launcher.launch(executableURL:arguments:)
+        )
+
+        try scheduler.scheduleRelaunch(after: .seconds(3))
+
+        XCTAssertEqual(launcher.executableURL?.path, "/bin/sh")
+        XCTAssertEqual(
+            launcher.arguments,
+            ["-c", "sleep 3; /usr/bin/open -n '/Applications/Ghost Pepper.app'"]
+        )
+    }
+

         hotkeyMonitor.startErrorSequence = [
@@ -452,9 +494,31 @@ private final class OnboardingSystemSettingsOpener: SystemSettingsOpening, @unch
 
 private final class OnboardingRelaunchScheduler: OnboardingRelaunchScheduling, @unchecked Sendable {
     private(set) var delays: [Duration] = []
+    var error: Error?
 
-    func scheduleRelaunch(after delay: Duration) {
+    func scheduleRelaunch(after delay: Duration) throws {
         delays.append(delay)
+        if let error {
+            throw error
+        }
+    }
+}
+
+private struct OnboardingRelaunchTestError: LocalizedError {
+    let description: String
+
+    var errorDescription: String? {
+        description
+    }
+}
+
+private final class OnboardingRelaunchLauncher: @unchecked Sendable {
+    private(set) var executableURL: URL?
+    private(set) var arguments: [String]?
+
+    func launch(executableURL: URL, arguments: [String]) throws {
+        self.executableURL = executableURL
+        self.arguments = arguments
     }
 }

@@ -0,0 +1,15 @@
+# Managed Deployment
+
+Ghost Pepper contains no MDM-specific logic. Managed-device behavior stays passive: macOS applies any administrator-provided permission pre-approvals, and the app simply observes the resulting system state.
+
+## Accessibility PPPC Reference
+
+Use these values when building a PPPC profile for managed deployments:
+
+| Field | Value |
+| --- | --- |
+| Bundle identifier | `com.github.matthartman.ghostpepper` |
+| Team identifier | `BBVMGXR9AY` |
+| Pre-approved permission | Accessibility |
+
+Accessibility is the only documented pre-approvable permission in this repository contract.

@@ -0,0 +1,16 @@
+# Ghost Pepper Release Notes
+
+## Requirements
+
+- macOS 14.0 Sonoma or later.
+- Apple Silicon hardware (M1 or later). Ghost Pepper relies on WhisperKit and FluidAudio for on-device ML inference, so the hardware requirement is derived from those dependencies rather than from an explicit project architecture constraint.
+
+## First-Launch Resource Expectations
+
+- Approximately 2 GB of RAM for the speech and cleanup models together.
+- Approximately 1.5 GB of disk space for model downloads on first launch.
+
+## Distribution Notes
+
+- Ghost Pepper stores runtime downloads and local data in `~/Library/Application Support/GhostPepper/`, so app updates replace the bundle without overwriting user data.
+- Managed deployments can pre-approve Accessibility through the PPPC values documented in `docs/managed-deployment.md`.

@@ -2,13 +2,16 @@
 
 1. Update the version string and build number in `Sources/App/Info.plist`.
 2. Run `scripts/build-dmg.sh`.
-3. Notarize the DMG with Apple before updating `appcast.xml`.
-4. Update `appcast.xml` with the new version, build number, DMG size, download URL, and EdDSA signature.
-5. Commit and push the `appcast.xml` change to `main`.
-6. Create the GitHub release with `gh release create`.
+3. Archive or export the notarized distribution build with hardened runtime re-enabled, for example `xcodebuild archive ... ENABLE_HARDENED_RUNTIME=YES`, before notarizing the release artifact.
+4. Notarize the DMG with Apple before updating `appcast.xml`.
+5. Update `appcast.xml` with the new version, build number, DMG size, download URL, and EdDSA signature.
+6. Commit and push the `appcast.xml` change to `main`.
+7. Create the GitHub release with `gh release create --notes-file docs/release-notes.md`.
 
 The DMG release remains a manual process. No CI/CD workflow is checked into this repository.
 
 The checked-in `appcast.xml` advertises a single signed Ghost Pepper release, and Sparkle uses the build number from that feed for version comparison.
 
 Update installs preserve user settings and local data because Ghost Pepper stores preferences in user defaults and runtime data under `~/Library/Application Support/GhostPepper`, outside the application bundle that Sparkle replaces.
+
+Use the release body in `docs/release-notes.md` so each published release carries the current macOS, Apple Silicon, RAM, and disk requirements. Managed Accessibility pre-approval stays documented separately in `docs/managed-deployment.md`.

@@ -0,0 +1,21 @@
+#!/bin/bash
+set -euo pipefail
+
+ROOT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
+MODEL_DIR="$ROOT_DIR/Resources/models"
+MODEL_NAME="ggml-small.en.bin"
+MODEL_URL="https://huggingface.co/ggerganov/whisper.cpp/resolve/main/$MODEL_NAME?download=true"
+TARGET_PATH="$MODEL_DIR/$MODEL_NAME"
+
+mkdir -p "$MODEL_DIR"
+
+if [ -f "$TARGET_PATH" ]; then
+  echo "Whisper model already present at $TARGET_PATH"
+  echo "Ghost Pepper downloads WhisperKit runtime models to ~/Library/Application Support/GhostPepper/ at runtime."
+  exit 0
+fi
+
+echo "Downloading whisper.cpp model to $TARGET_PATH..."
+curl --fail --location --output "$TARGET_PATH" "$MODEL_URL"
+echo "Downloaded $TARGET_PATH"
+echo "Ghost Pepper downloads WhisperKit runtime models to ~/Library/Application Support/GhostPepper/ at runtime."
